Title: PpNUDX8, a Peach NUDIX Hydrolase, Plays a Negative Regulator in Response to Drought Stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional characterization of PpNUDX8 was performed mainly in heterologous systems (apple calli and Nicotiana tabacum), with no PpNUDX8 loss-of-function mutants reported. Drought stress was simulated using PEG6000 rather than field water-deficit conditions.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PpNUDX8 encodes a NUDIX hydrolase (Prupe.5G062300.1) with a typical NUDIX domain and its promoter contains cis-elements associated with ABA responsiveness, drought inducibility, gibberellin and methyl jasmonate responsiveness, and light responsiveness.
2. PpNUDX8 transcript levels in peach seedlings are induced by 15% PEG6000-simulated drought and by ABA treatment, indicating responsiveness to drought and ABA signals.
3. Overexpression of PpNUDX8 in Wanglin apple calli reduces callus growth, decreases fresh weight, and increases MDA content under 4% PEG6000 treatment, indicating reduced drought tolerance.
4. Overexpression of PpNUDX8 in Nicotiana tabacum results in greater wilting, shorter primary roots, lower chlorophyll, reduced proline and soluble protein contents, and higher MDA content and relative conductivity under PEG6000-induced drought, demonstrating that PpNUDX8 negatively regulates drought tolerance.
5. In transgenic tobacco, PpNUDX8 overexpression alters NAD and NADH levels and the NAD/NADH ratio and modifies expression of the NAD biosynthetic enzyme QS2, indicating that PpNUDX8 disrupts NAD/NADH homeostasis, especially under drought stress.
6. PpNUDX8 overexpression reduces ABA sensitivity in tobacco, conferring higher seed germination and green cotyledon rates and longer primary roots under ABA, and decreases ABA content before and after drought treatment.
7. PpNUDX8 overexpression suppresses ABA signaling and stress-responsive gene expression by lowering SnRK2.2 expression, increasing PP2C6 and PP2C53 expression, and reducing drought-induced expression of DREBA1, RAB18, RD29A, RD29B, and LEA5.
8. PpNUDX8 overexpression decreases expression of the ABA biosynthesis gene NCED6 while increasing expression of the ABA catabolism gene CYP1, contributing to lower ABA levels in transgenic tobacco.
9. PpNUDX8 physically interacts with PpSnRk1γ, as shown by yeast two-hybrid and pull-down assays, suggesting integration into SnRK1-related signaling under drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PpNUDX8 acts as a negative regulator of drought stress tolerance and ABA signaling by disturbing NAD/NADH homeostasis, lowering ABA content, and suppressing ABA and stress-responsive gene expression, leading to reduced drought tolerance when overexpressed.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As a NUDIX hydrolase, PpNUDX8 perturbs NAD/NADH homeostasis, likely by affecting expression of the NAD biosynthetic enzyme QS2; it negatively regulates ABA signaling by decreasing SnRK2.2 expression and increasing PP2C6 and PP2C53 expression; it reduces ABA content by repressing NCED6 and enhancing CYP1 expression; and it physically interacts with the signaling component PpSnRk1γ.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling pathway and drought stress response pathways, including both ABA-dependent and ABA-independent (DREBA1-mediated) stress-responsive pathways linked to NAD/NADH homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: NAD/NADH homeostasis and ABA metabolism (affecting NAD biosynthesis via QS2 and ABA biosynthesis/degradation via NCED6 and CYP1)
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of transcription under drought)--> SnRK2.2; PpNUDX8 --(positive regulation of transcription under drought)--> PP2C6; PpNUDX8 --(positive regulation of transcription under drought)--> PP2C53; PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of drought-induced transcription)--> DREBA1; PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of drought-induced transcription)--> RAB18; PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of drought-induced transcription)--> RD29A; PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of drought-induced transcription)--> RD29B; PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of drought-induced transcription)--> LEA5; PpNUDX8 --(negative regulation of transcription)--> NCED6; PpNUDX8 --(positive regulation of transcription)--> CYP1; PpNUDX8 --(modulation of transcription)--> QS2 (higher QS2 expression in transgenic lines under control and stronger downregulation after drought).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NUDIX hydrolase containing a typical conserved NUDIX motif (GX5EX7REUXEEXGU).
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PpSnRk1γ (Prupe.6G323700.1), interaction confirmed by yeast two-hybrid and pull-down assays.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: PpNUDX8 transcript levels in peach seedlings are induced by 15% PEG6000-simulated drought and by ABA treatment.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-simulated drought (4% PEG6000 in MS medium and 15% PEG6000 solution) and ABA treatments (1 μM and 10 μM ABA in MS for germination and seedling growth, 50 μmol/L ABA spray on peach seedlings).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought stress tolerance, ABA signaling, and NAD/NADH homeostasis regulated by a peach NUDIX hydrolase.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance) and ABA response.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 4% PEG6000, total chlorophyll content in PpNUDX8 transgenic tobacco leaves was 1.53–1.64 mg/g compared with 2.214266 mg/g in WT. PpNUDX8 transgenic tobacco showed significantly shorter primary roots than WT under 4% PEG6000 and 10 μM ABA. After 15% PEG6000 treatment for 4 h, PpNUDX8 transgenic tobacco displayed higher MDA content and relative conductivity and lower proline and soluble protein contents than WT. Under 1 μM ABA, PpNUDX8 transgenic seeds exhibited higher germination rates and a higher percentage of green cotyledons than WT. PpNUDX8 transgenic lines had lower ABA content than WT both before and after 15% PEG6000 treatment, and showed higher NAD content and NAD/NADH ratio but lower NADH content than WT under control conditions, with stronger decreases in NAD and NADH after 15% PEG6000.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In transgenic tobacco, PpNUDX8 overexpression leads to shorter primary roots under 4% PEG6000, reduced chlorophyll content under drought, decreased accumulation of osmotic regulators (proline and soluble protein) under 15% PEG6000, increased membrane damage (higher MDA content and relative conductivity), reduced ABA sensitivity (higher seed germination and green cotyledon percentage and longer primary roots under ABA), altered NAD and NADH levels and NAD/NADH ratio, and altered expression of QS2, SnRK2.2, PP2C6, PP2C53, DREBA1, RAB18, RD29A, RD29B, and LEA5.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Peach (seedlings), Wanglin apple (Malus domestica) calli, Nicotiana tabacum wild-type and three PpNUDX8 overexpression lines (OE-1, OE-2, OE-3).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of full-length PpNUDX8 CDS from peach cDNA, promoter cis-element analysis, protein multiple sequence alignment and phylogenetic analysis, qRT-PCR expression profiling under 15% PEG6000 and ABA in peach seedlings, Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of apple calli and tobacco with 35S::PpNUDX8, PEG6000-simulated drought assays on calli and seedlings, measurement of fresh weight, primary root length, chlorophyll, soluble protein, proline, MDA content and relative conductivity, ABA content determination by HPLC-MS/MS, NAD and NADH quantification by ELISA-based kit, RT-qPCR analysis of QS2, SnRK2.2, PP2C6, PP2C53, DREBA1, RAB18, RD29A, RD29B, LEA5, NCED6, and CYP1, yeast two-hybrid assays, and in vitro pull-down assays.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in Wanglin apple calli and Nicotiana tabacum (35S::PpNUDX8), PEG6000-simulated drought assays, ABA sensitivity tests, physiological and biochemical measurements, gene expression analysis by qRT-PCR, and protein–protein interaction verified by yeast two-hybrid and pull-down assays.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the peach NUDIX hydrolase family (Phytozome Prupe locus) and cloned from a peach cDNA library, followed by sequence alignment and promoter cis-element analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because PpNUDX8 acts as a negative regulator of drought tolerance and ABA signaling, reducing or knocking out PpNUDX8 activity in peach or related horticultural crops may enhance drought resistance, improve osmotic adjustment capacity, and increase ABA accumulation under stress; PpNUDX8 provides a candidate target gene for genetic engineering-based drought improvement in peach.
